sourceMovie.title: Troll
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Troll for the same reason it broke through in 2022: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Roar Uthaug keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The fantasy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Troll clicked for you, The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Troll?
faq.answer: Try The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring, The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey, The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug—each shares DNA with Troll while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Troll?
faq.answer: Troll (2022) is best described as Fantasy, Action, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Troll part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Troll (2022) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
